purpose: Decomposes the yield curve into expectations and term premium components to explain why different Treasury maturities exhibit different correlations with equities
components: Short end (2Y): dominated by expected policy rate path (Taylor Rule), countercyclical under credible CB → negative correlation with equities
Long end (30Y): dominated by term premium (fiscal supply, inflation risk premium), procyclical under current fiscal policy → positive correlation with equities
Four historical precedent episodes with resolution timelines of 3-14 months
Resolution mechanism: policy intervention (fiscal consolidation or central bank backstop) required in 3 of 4 cases
current_assessment: The bifurcation is a robust empirical phenomenon but its interpretation as a fiscal dominance signal is contested. The internal contradiction (Fed simultaneously credible at short end and impotent at long end) has alternative explanations: QT mechanics, supply-demand imbalance, foreign investor rebalancing, inflation risk premium. Without policy intervention, the current bifurcation may persist longer than any precedent.
limitations: Interpretation is contested — fiscal dominance vs transient explanations
Internal logical contradiction identified by challenger_01
Resolution mechanism depends on policy intervention that has no clear path currently
Four precedent episodes is a small sample with different macro contexts
